deep_dive.title: CACR:Reinforcing Temporal Answer Grounding in Instructional Video via Candidate-Aware Causal Reasoning
deep_dive.abstract: The task of temporal answer grounding in instructional video (TAGV), which aims to locate precise video segments that respond to natural language queries, is increasingly important for direct video answer retrieval. This task remains challenging due to the need to comprehend semantically complex questions and to address the significant length mismatch between untrimmed videos and short target moments. Existing methods often suffer from sensitivity to irrelevant content or insufficient visual reasoning capabilities. To tackle these limitations, we propose a Candidate-Aware Causal Reasoning (CACR) framework. Our approach first employs a Visual-Language Pre-training based Candidate Selection (VBCS) algorithm to efficiently generate K candidate segments, then applies a temporal logic reasoning module enhanced by a rejection reward mechanism and optimized via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for robust inference. Extensive experiments on six benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU), providing a new perspective for reasoning-based retrieval in long videos.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Candidate-Aware Causal Reasoning for temporal answer grounding in instructional video
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于候选感知因果推理的教学视频时间答案定位
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于候选感知因果推理的教学视频时间答案定位
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于候选感知因果推理的教学视频时间答案定位
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CACR framework that integrates candidate selection with causal reasoning to precisely locate video segments for natural language queries, improving robustness to irrelevant content and handling length mismatch.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对教学视频中时间答案定位任务对无关内容敏感和视觉推理能力不足的问题，提出CACR框架，结合候选感知选择与因果推理，有效处理视频长度不匹配并提升定位的准确性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对教学视频中时间答案定位任务对无关内容敏感和视觉推理能力不足的问题，提出CACR框架，结合候选感知选择与因果推理，有效处理视频长度不匹配并提升定位的准确性。

deep_dive.title: OR-Action: Multi-Role Video Understanding with Fine-Grained Actions
deep_dive.abstract: Fine-grained understanding of operating room (OR) activity could enable workflow-aware assistance, yet remains difficult due to clutter, occlusions, and limited sensing. The prevailing approach to model this environment is scene graphs as an interpretable representation of OR interactions. Converting their frame-wise relational predictions into temporally extended, fine-grained actions however, is challenging without explicit temporal modeling. To enable a principled temporal evaluation of current OR understanding methods, we introduce the first action-centric benchmark built on a publicly available ego-exocentric OR dataset by defining a fine-grained, multi-role action taxonomy and generating dense action segments via distillation from ground-truth scene graph state changes. Experiments on this benchmark show that current scene graph prediction methods struggle to model temporal structure, even when adding explicit modeling through Graph Neural Networks. We therefore introduce a vision-only temporal model that outperforms graph-based methods significantly when using all available egocentric video as input. Building on this model we also introduce a novel multi- to single-view feature alignment strategy that improves single-view performance on multi-role action recognition, mitigating the need for extensive egocentric video capture. Benchmark and code will be released upon acceptance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Fine-grained understanding of operating room (OR) video with action-centric benchmark
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 手术室视频的细粒度动作理解及动作中心基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 手术室视频的细粒度动作理解及动作中心基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 手术室视频的细粒度动作理解及动作中心基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces OR-Action, the first action-centric benchmark for multi-role operating room video understanding, defining fine-grained action taxonomy and generating dense action segments via scene graph distillation to enable workflow-aware assistance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为手术室视频理解提出首个以动作为中心的基准OR-Action，定义细粒度多角色动作分类，通过场景图蒸馏生成密集动作片段，为工作流感知的辅助提供标准化评估框架。

deep_dive.title: 4DP-QA: Scalable QA for 4D Perception in Vision Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Despite recent advances, Vision Language Models (VLMs) still struggle to grasp the dynamics of the world. We note that the ability to reason about a 4D scene, challenging in itself, is further complicated by two factors. First, VLMs observe motion indirectly via its projection onto 2D images. Second, existing datasets fail to disentangle object and camera motion. To address these challenges, we present a QA generation pipeline that focuses on motion-related scene understanding. We take particular care of the entanglement of camera and object motion by casting tracking in both the traditional way and in a novel, fixed reference system, dubbed True-Motion Tracking, which provides an intuitive description of motion. From this pipeline, we generate a large-scale training dataset of 400K samples, 4DP-QA (4D Perception QA), and a 2.2K-sample benchmark, 4DP-QA-Bench. Training existing models on our dataset yields performance improvements on an external benchmark, validating the effectiveness of our method.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: creates QA pipeline with True-Motion Tracking to improve VLM understanding of dynamic 4D scenes
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 构建QA流水线，通过真实运动跟踪提升VLM对4D动态场景理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 构建QA流水线，通过真实运动跟踪提升VLM对4D动态场景理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 构建QA流水线，通过真实运动跟踪提升VLM对4D动态场景理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: 4DP-QA creates a QA pipeline with True-Motion Tracking to improve Vision Language Models' understanding of dynamic 4D scenes, yielding a large-scale dataset and better motion reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉语言模型难以理解动态场景的问题，该工作提出4DP-QA流水线，创新性引入真实运动跟踪以解耦相机与物体运动，生成大规模运动相关问答数据。实验表明，在该数据上训练可显著提升VLM对4D场景的推理能力。该工作强调无需额外模型结构修改，仅通过定制数据集即可增强VLMs的时空理解，为视频理解的scalable监督提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对视觉语言模型难以理解动态场景的问题，该工作提出4DP-QA流水线，创新性引入真实运动跟踪以解耦相机与物体运动，生成大规模运动相关问答数据。实验表明，在该数据上训练可显著提升VLM对4D场景的推理能力。该工作强调无需额外模型结构修改，仅通过定制数据集即可增强VLMs的时空理解，为视频理解的scalable监督提供了新方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: vision language model for video understanding

quick_skim.title: Late-Layer Fusion is Enough: Dual-Path Vision Token Routing for Multimodal Large Language Models under Visual Saturation
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) commonly inherit the deep, symmetric Transformer backbone designed for unimodal text modeling, and apply the same computation uniformly to image and language tokens. This design overlooks a key modality asymmetry: image and text tokens differ substantially in information density, redundancy, and required reasoning depth. Through a layer-wise analysis of LLaVA-1.5, we observe that vision tokens tend to saturate in the middle layers. Specifically, text-to-image attention decreases from 0.68 at layer 0 to 0.07 by layer 4, and stabilizes near 0.04 after layer 18, whereas text tokens continue to benefit from deep semantic processing. These findings suggest a mismatch between architectural symmetry and depth-asynchronous modality evolution, resulting in redundant visual computation and possible drift in perceptual representations during deep task-specific adaptation. Motivated by this, we propose Dual-Path Vision Token Routing (DPVR), a modality-asymmetric routing framework for efficient MLLMs. Its core instantiation, DPVR-LF (Late-Layer Fusion), routes vision tokens at the saturation point into a one-layer trainable side branch, runs a thirteen-layer text-only forward that skips image positions in the deep stack, and re-fuses the visual and textual streams only at the final layer. With approximately 3% trainable parameters, DPVR-LF preserves competitive multimodal performance on standard benchmarks while reducing visual computation in the deep Transformer stack. The results challenge the conventional assumption that vision tokens must traverse all deep language-model layers, and indicate that a single late fusion layer can be sufficient for maintaining strong perceptual competence in LLaVA-style MLLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Dual-path vision token routing based on visual saturation for efficient MLLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于视觉饱和度的双路径令牌路由，提升多模态大模型效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于视觉饱和度的双路径令牌路由，提升多模态大模型效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于视觉饱和度的双路径令牌路由，提升多模态大模型效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes a late-layer fusion strategy and dual-path token routing for MLLMs, leveraging the observation that vision tokens saturate early, to reduce computation while preserving text reasoning depth.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型通常对图文令牌施加相同的深层计算，但视觉令牌在中间层即趋于饱和。本文提出晚期融合和双路径路由策略，让视觉令牌在饱和层后绕过深层处理，仅文本令牌继续深度推理，从而大幅提升推理效率。实验证明在保持性能的同时显著降低了计算成本。该发现为不对称模态处理提供了理论依据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态大模型通常对图文令牌施加相同的深层计算，但视觉令牌在中间层即趋于饱和。本文提出晚期融合和双路径路由策略，让视觉令牌在饱和层后绕过深层处理，仅文本令牌继续深度推理，从而大幅提升推理效率。实验证明在保持性能的同时显著降低了计算成本。该发现为不对称模态处理提供了理论依据。

quick_skim.title: MultiToP: Learning to Patch Visual Tokens to Mitigate Hallucinations in Video Large Multimodal Models
quick_skim.abstract: Video Large Multimodal Models have achieved remarkable progress in video understanding, yet they remain prone to hallucinations, where generated responses are not faithfully supported by the input video. In this paper, we propose MultiToP, a multimodal-context-aware visual token patching framework that mitigates hallucinations by refining unreliable visual tokens before language generation. MultiToP introduces a lightweight Visual Token Patcher to predict token-level replacement distributions and selectively substitute unreliable visual tokens with a dynamic global patch token. To train the patcher effectively, we further propose information-guided rank calibration, which uses answer-conditioned frame-level information cues derived from the backbone to guide token replacement. Combined with ground-truth answer supervision and sparsity regularization, MultiToP enables localized visual evidence refinement without modifying the original model. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MultiToP effectively reduces hallucinations on Vript-HAL with negligible inference overhead, improving the F1 scores of Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct by 50.60% over the vanilla model. Meanwhile, MultiToP preserves general video understanding ability, yielding an 18.58% relative accuracy gain on ActivityNet-QA for Video-LLaVA-7B.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Mitigates hallucinations in Video Large Multimodal Models for video understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 缓解视频大语言模型中的幻觉以提升视频理解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 缓解视频大语言模型中的幻觉以提升视频理解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 缓解视频大语言模型中的幻觉以提升视频理解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MultiToP, a lightweight visual token patching framework to reduce hallucinations in video VLMs by predicting and substituting unreliable tokens, trained with information-guided rank calibration, improving response faithfulness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频大语言模型中存在的幻觉问题，提出MultiToP框架，通过学习预测并替换不可靠的视觉令牌来减少幻觉，结合信息引导的排名校准进行训练，从而提升视频理解中的响应忠实度，为构建更可靠的多模态视频模型提供了基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视频大语言模型中存在的幻觉问题，提出MultiToP框架，通过学习预测并替换不可靠的视觉令牌来减少幻觉，结合信息引导的排名校准进行训练，从而提升视频理解中的响应忠实度，为构建更可靠的多模态视频模型提供了基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: vision language model for video understanding

quick_skim.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) project images into hundreds to thousands of visual tokens, making decoder inference expensive in both attention computation and KV-cache memory. Existing visual-token reduction methods largely follow a rank-and-remove paradigm: they score visual tokens, keep a compact subset, and permanently discard the rest. We show that this irreversible action is fragile because visual-token importance changes across decoder depth; tokens ranked low at one stage may become relevant in later layers, especially for grounding-sensitive queries. We propose Reroute, a training-free plug-in that replaces removal with recoverable routing. At each routing stage, selected vision tokens pass through decoder blocks, while deferred tokens bypass the stage and re-enter the candidate pool at the next routing decision. Reroute reuses existing attention-score ranking rules and stage-wise schedules, preserving the theoretical TFLOPs and KV-cache budget class of the pruning method it augments. Across FastV, PDrop, and Nüwa variants on LLaVA-1.5 and Qwen backbones, reroute improves grounding under aggressive token reduction while maintaining general VQA performance. These results suggest that VLM token reduction should not be viewed only as irreversible pruning, but also as recoverable routing. The code can be found here: https://github.com/elmma/mllm-reroute/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Training-free recoverable visual token routing for efficient VLM inference, beneficial for grounding-sensitive queries
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 免训练可恢复视觉令牌路由，提升VLM效率并利于定位敏感查询
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 免训练可恢复视觉令牌路由，提升VLM效率并利于定位敏感查询
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 免训练可恢复视觉令牌路由，提升VLM效率并利于定位敏感查询
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reroute replaces irreversible visual token removal with recoverable routing in VLMs, preserving token access across layers to improve efficiency and accuracy for grounding-sensitive tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有VLM视觉令牌削减方法采用不可逆删除，导致后期令牌重要性变化时信息丢失。Reroute提出免训练的可恢复路由机制，将未选中的令牌暂存并在需要时重新引入，保证定位敏感查询的性能。实验显示在保持精度的同时显著降低了推理成本。该技术为VLM的高效部署提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有VLM视觉令牌削减方法采用不可逆删除，导致后期令牌重要性变化时信息丢失。Reroute提出免训练的可恢复路由机制，将未选中的令牌暂存并在需要时重新引入，保证定位敏感查询的性能。实验显示在保持精度的同时显著降低了推理成本。该技术为VLM的高效部署提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Rethinking RAG in Long Videos: What to Retrieve and How to Use It?
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation is moving beyond text into long, egocentric video, where systems must select query-relevant chunks across multiple modalities and temporal granularities. Yet progress in VideoRAG is limited by two gaps: existing benchmarks allow queries to be answered without the video, obscuring retrieval errors, and prior methods apply a single modality-granularity configuration per query, ignoring chunk-level variability. We address both by introducing V-RAGBench, a benchmark of $\langle$query, evidence chunk, answer$\rangle$ triplets that enables faithful, decoupled evaluation of retrieval and generation, and CARVE, a simple method that runs parallel retrievers across configurations and employs chunk-adaptive reranking to identify the winning configuration for each chunk. Each chunk then enters the generator under its winning configuration selected during retrieval, yielding an interleaved evidence form where the chunk-level decision propagates across both stages. CARVE outperforms eight recent VideoRAG baselines, with the chunks supplied to the generator interleaving multiple configurations rather than sharing a single one, a behavior unattainable by query-level methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient retrieval-augmented generation for long video understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 高效检索增强生成长视频理解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 高效检索增强生成长视频理解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 高效检索增强生成长视频理解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces V-RAGBench for faithful evaluation of VideoRAG and CARVE, a chunk-adaptive reranking method that improves retrieval and generation for query-answering in long egocentric videos.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对长视频RAG中检索与生成评估不可靠问题，提出V-RAGBench基准和CARVE方法。V-RAGBench提供查询-证据片段-答案三元组实现解耦评估；CARVE并行运行多配置检索器并采用片段自适应重排序，以识别最优窗口。实验证明该方法提升长视频问答的准确性和效率，为VideoRAG奠定可靠评估基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对长视频RAG中检索与生成评估不可靠问题，提出V-RAGBench基准和CARVE方法。V-RAGBench提供查询-证据片段-答案三元组实现解耦评估；CARVE并行运行多配置检索器并采用片段自适应重排序，以识别最优窗口。实验证明该方法提升长视频问答的准确性和效率，为VideoRAG奠定可靠评估基础。

quick_skim.title: HYDRA-X: Native Unified Multimodal Models with Holistic Visual Tokenizers
quick_skim.abstract: Holistic visual tokenizers are fundamental to unified multimodal models (UMMs) as they map diverse visual inputs into a unified representation space. In this paper, we present HYDRA-X, the first UMM that unifies image and video tokenization within a single Vision Transformer (ViT). Our design is driven by two core challenges: efficiently injecting spatiotemporal reconstruction capability into a native ViT, and embedding image- and video-level semantic awareness into the latent space. To address the first, comprehensive ablations reveal two key findings: (1) frame-level causal temporal attention suffices for visual reconstruction, whereas full spatiotemporal attention degrades it; and (2) hierarchical temporal compression substantially outperforms single-step alternatives. To tackle the second, we propose a lightweight decompressor that upsamples temporally compressed features under joint image-video teacher supervision, thereby enforcing complementary semantic structures within the compact latent space. Building on this holistic tokenizer, we further propose a principled improvement of the editing pipeline: source-target interaction should occur at the latent level inside the tokenizer rather than at the semantic level inside the LLM, substantially improving editing consistency and accelerating convergence. Instantiated at the 7B dense model, HYDRA-X achieves strong performance across image and video understanding and generation tasks, paving the way for future unified-tokenizer UMMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unifies image/video tokenization with efficient frame-level causal attention and hierarchical temporal compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 统一图像视频分词，帧级因果注意力与层次化时序压缩实现高效视频表示
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 统一图像视频分词，帧级因果注意力与层次化时序压缩实现高效视频表示
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 统一图像视频分词，帧级因果注意力与层次化时序压缩实现高效视频表示
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HYDRA-X proposes a unified Vision Transformer tokenizer for both images and videos with efficient temporal attention and compression, enabling native multimodal models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对统一图像和视频分词的关键挑战，提出原生视觉Transformer设计，采用帧级因果时序注意力和层次化时序压缩，高效实现时空重建，为原生多模态模型提供了高效的基础分词器。经消融实验验证，因果时序注意力足以胜任视觉重建，层次化压缩显著优于简单下采样。该分词器可无缝应用于统一多模态模型中，推动视频理解与生成的一体化发展。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该工作针对统一图像和视频分词的关键挑战，提出原生视觉Transformer设计，采用帧级因果时序注意力和层次化时序压缩，高效实现时空重建，为原生多模态模型提供了高效的基础分词器。经消融实验验证，因果时序注意力足以胜任视觉重建，层次化压缩显著优于简单下采样。该分词器可无缝应用于统一多模态模型中，推动视频理解与生成的一体化发展。

quick_skim.title: Iterative Visual Thinking: Teaching Vision-Language Models Spatial Self-Correction through Visual Feedback
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) achieve strong singleshot spatial grounding, yet lack any mechanism to observe and correct their own predictions. We find that naively prompting a VLM to iterate over rendered visualizations of its predictions causes catastrophic failure: Acc@0.5 on referring expression comprehension collapses from 79.6% to 48.7% (a 31 percentage point drop), revealing a fundamental gap between grounding capability and self-correction ability. We propose Iterative Visual Thinking (IVT), a closed-loop framework in which the model predicts a bounding box, observes the prediction rendered on the image, and iteratively refines through visual feedback. A two-phase training recipe closes the self-correction gap: first, we exploit the base model's own predictions as realistic errors and prompt a teacher VLM to generate corrective reasoning traces, yielding supervised data without human annotation; second, we apply Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a simple IoU reward to stabilize multi-step refinement. On a mixed benchmark spanning RefCOCOg, Ref-Adv, and Ref-L4 (505 test samples), SFT warm-up with IVT surpasses the single-shot base model on every metric: Acc@0.5 rises to 82.0% (+2.4pp), Acc@0.7 to 74.1% (+3.2pp), and Acc@0.9 to 48.3% (+2.8pp). GRPO further reduces per-step IoU degradation by 5x, stabilizing the refinement trajectory. All training uses only 2,400 samples on a single GPU, demonstrating that spatial self-correction is a learnable capability that can be instilled at modest scale.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: iterative spatial grounding and self-correction with VLMs, method transferable to temporal video grounding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 迭代空间定位与VLM自校正，方法可迁移至视频时间定位
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 迭代空间定位与VLM自校正，方法可迁移至视频时间定位
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 迭代空间定位与VLM自校正，方法可迁移至视频时间定位
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes IVT, a closed-loop framework where a VLM iteratively refines bounding box predictions via visual feedback, closing the self-correction gap; technique applicable to video grounding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对VLM在空间定位后缺乏自我修正能力的问题，提出迭代视觉思维框架，模型预测边界框后观察渲染结果并通过视觉反馈迭代精炼。通过两阶段训练缩小自校正差距。该方法为时空定位提供了可迁移的闭环修正范式，可应用于视频时间定位任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对VLM在空间定位后缺乏自我修正能力的问题，提出迭代视觉思维框架，模型预测边界框后观察渲染结果并通过视觉反馈迭代精炼。通过两阶段训练缩小自校正差距。该方法为时空定位提供了可迁移的闭环修正范式，可应用于视频时间定位任务。
